stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
The mention of Meta's Llama series indicates relevance to major advancements in large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: This could be Windows’ M1 moment — but expect it to cost a ton
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: Nvidia is entering the consumer laptop chip market with RTX Spark.
The announcement is significant as it challenges the dominance of traditional x86 architectures in Windows laptops.
The article frames this as a potential 'M1 moment' for Windows, referencing Apple's success with efficient Arm-based chips.

stories.title: Meta’s own AI was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Meta's AI support chatbot was exploited by hackers to compromise Instagram accounts.
The attack involved tricking the chatbot into changing the associated email address for a target profile.
This vulnerability highlights significant security risks within large-scale AI customer service implementations.

stories.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Anthropic has officially filed with the SEC to begin its Initial Public Offering (IPO).
This filing marks a significant development in the race between major AI labs (like OpenAI and Anthropic) to go public.
The event signals a major financial and industry shift in the highly competitive generative AI sector.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman after multiple ChatGPT-linked murders
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Florida is suing OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman over alleged ChatGPT-linked murders.
The lawsuit claims Altman demonstrates an 'utter disregard' for human lives.
The story addresses serious legal and ethical implications surrounding AI deployment.

stories.title: An OpenAI model solved a famous math problem that stumped humans for 80 years
stories.author: Kai Williams
bullet_points: An OpenAI model reportedly solved a complex mathematical problem that has eluded human mathematicians for eight decades.
The article suggests the AI's solution represents a significant computational or algorithmic breakthrough.
The piece is framed as an analysis/critique of OpenAI's own explanation of the solution.

stories.title: Alphabet Announces $80B Equity Capital Raise to Expand AI Infra and Compute
stories.author: gregschlom
bullet_points: Alphabet is undertaking a massive $80 billion equity capital raise.
The primary purpose of the funding is to expand AI infrastructure and compute capabilities.
This signals a major, sustained investment push into AI hardware and scaling by a tech giant.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the DeepSeek-V3 family, indicating relevance in the competitive LLM space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The mention of Bloom indicates ongoing interest in large-scale language models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, enabling AI processing to run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is the zero-configuration setup, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: From 15 hours to one minute: How AI/ML is speeding up GM's development
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: GM is leveraging AI/ML to drastically accelerate the vehicle development lifecycle, reducing time from 15 hours to one minute in certain processes.
The shift involves extensive virtualization in car manufacturing, utilizing technologies like CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) and FEA (Finite Element Analysis).
The adoption of digital twins is a key component of this modernization effort within the automotive industry.

stories.title: Nvidia RTX Spark comes to Windows PCs with Arm CPU, RTX GPU, and unified memory
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Nvidia is introducing RTX Spark technology for Windows PCs.
This new hardware configuration utilizes Arm CPUs, RTX GPUs, and unified memory.
Initial deployment will focus on laptop workstations and mini desktop PCs.

stories.title: Intel: Our upcoming AI chip will be cheaper, run cooler than Nvidia, AMD options
stories.author: Financial Times
bullet_points: Intel is developing an upcoming AI chip designed to compete directly with Nvidia and AMD.
The chip, named Crescent Island, emphasizes cost-effectiveness and lower power consumption (cooler operation).
Key technical features include air-cooling and the use of LPDDR5 memory.

stories.title: Stateful Online Monitoring Catches Distributed Agent Attacks
stories.author: Hamed Hassani
bullet_points: Distributed agent attacks pose a significant threat as attackers split harmful tasks across multiple benign-looking accounts.
Current safety monitors are structurally blind to these attacks because they analyze agent contexts in isolation, not in aggregate.
The research proposes a solution using 'Stateful Online Monitoring' to detect misuse that is only visible across multiple accounts.

stories.title: Vision-Language Models Suppress Female Representations Under Ambiguous Input
stories.author: Mahzarin R. Banaji
bullet_points: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit gender bias when presented with ambiguous visual input.
Despite alignment efforts, models default to male representations for occupations when gender is not clearly visible.
The study highlights a critical gap in current VLM research regarding handling real-world, ambiguous scenarios.

stories.title: RayDer: Scalable Self-Supervised Novel View Synthesis from Real-World Video
stories.author: Björn Ommer
bullet_points: Introduces RayDer, a unified, feed-forward transformer for Self-Supervised Novel View Synthesis (NVS).
Addresses scalability challenges in NVS by consolidating camera estimation, scene reconstruction, and rendering into one backbone.
Transforms the complex NVS problem into a well-posed, single-model scaling challenge.

stories.title: Vision-Language Models Suppress Female Representations Under Ambiguous Input
stories.author: Mahzarin R. Banaji
bullet_points: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit gender bias when presented with ambiguous visual inputs.
Despite alignment training, models default to male representations for occupations when gender is unclear.
The study highlights a critical gap in understanding how VLMs handle real-world, non-explicit demographic scenarios.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over AI risks
stories.author: cyunker
bullet_points: Florida's Attorney General has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman.
The lawsuit centers on alleged harms associated with the use of Artificial Intelligence.
This signals growing regulatory and legal scrutiny of major AI developers.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google has introduced a new AI agent named Gemini Spark, advertised as a 24/7 task-handling assistant.
The Verge review suggests the agent can be 'shockingly good' at performing tasks.
The article raises concerns regarding the financial cost and potential privacy implications of using Spark.

stories.title: Lumos-Nexus: Efficient Frequency Bridging with Homogeneous Latent Space for Video Unified Models
stories.author: Yong Liu
bullet_points: Lumos-Nexus addresses the computational bottleneck in training high-fidelity, unified video generation models.
It proposes a training-efficient framework to integrate large generators while maintaining strong reasoning capabilities.
The core innovation involves using 'Efficient Frequency Bridging with Homogeneous Latent Space'.

stories.title: TunerDiT: Training-free Progressive Steering of Diffusion Transformer for Multi-Event Video Generation
stories.author: Volker Tresp
bullet_points: Introduces TunerDiT, a new method for progressive steering in Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) for video generation.
Addresses the challenge of generating long-horizon videos with multiple events in Text-to-Video (T2V) models.
Leverages intrinsic turning points in the DiT denoising trajectory to control generation from global layout to fine details without retraining.

stories.title: Language Models Learn Constructional Semantics, Not To Mention Syntax: Investigating LM Understanding of Paired-Focus Constructions
stories.author: Leonie Weissweiler
bullet_points: The research investigates whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can learn the semantics of rare grammatical constructions, specifically Paired-Focus constructions in English.
It addresses the open question of whether open-source models possess robust constructional understanding, a capability often attributed only to the largest proprietary LLMs.
A novel dataset was created to test the meaning acquisition of these rare constructions using various models.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the core LLM challenge of long-context reasoning, where models struggle with extensive, distracting information.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve reasoning.
Overcomes limitations of prior work by providing supervision on intermediate reasoning steps, rather than just sparse, final outcomes.

stories.title: What Gets Unmasked First? Trajectory Analysis of Diffusion Models for Graph-to-Text Generation
stories.author: Chengkai Li
bullet_points: Introduces a systematic study of Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) specifically for graph-to-text generation.
Finds that MDLMs prioritize generating entities first, followed by relational/function words, contrasting with linear autoregressive LLMs.
Identifies a previously undocumented failure mode related to supervised fine-tuning in these models.

stories.title: Positional versus Symbolic Attention Heads: Learning Dynamics, RoPE Geometry, and Length Generalization
stories.author: Cristobal Rojas
bullet_points: The research investigates the learning dynamics of attention heads in Transformer models (like GPT-J) to improve understanding of their behavior.
It compares positional reasoning (number tasks) against symbolic reasoning (letter tasks) within structurally equivalent multi-hop reasoning problems.
The study aims to enhance the predictability and safe deployment of large language models by understanding how they handle different types of input.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-context reasoning in LLMs, where models struggle with extensive, distracting information.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve reasoning.
Overcomes limitations of prior methods by providing supervision for intermediate reasoning steps, rather than just relying on sparse, outcome-only rewards.

stories.title: Giving Sensors a Voice: Multimodal JEPA for Semantic Time-Series Embeddings
stories.author: Henrik Ohlsson
bullet_points: Introduces CHARM, a new model for general-purpose representation learning on heterogeneous multivariate time series.
CHARM integrates channel-level textual descriptions into a Transformer encoder, making it equivariant to channel order.
The model is trained using a Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) with a novel loss function for temporal stability.

stories.title: Functional Attention: From Pairwise Affinities to Functional Correspondences
stories.author: Daniel Cremers
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of operator learning, which involves mapping between infinite-dimensional function spaces.
Critiques existing transformer-based operators for relying on token-wise attention, which ignores global functional structure.
Proposes 'Functional Attention,' a novel mechanism that reinterprets attention as a functional correspondence between adaptive bases.

stories.title: Positional versus Symbolic Attention Heads: Learning Dynamics, RoPE Geometry, and Length Generalization
stories.author: Cristobal Rojas
bullet_points: The paper investigates the learning dynamics of attention heads in Transformer models (like GPT-J) to improve understanding of their behavior.
It compares positional reasoning (number tasks) against symbolic reasoning (letter tasks) within the attention mechanism.
The research aims to enhance the predictability and safe deployment of large language models, particularly regarding length generalization.

stories.title: Language Models Learn Constructional Semantics, Not To Mention Syntax: Investigating LM Understanding of Paired-Focus Constructions
stories.author: Leonie Weissweiler
bullet_points: The research investigates whether large language models (LLMs) truly grasp the semantics of rare, complex grammatical constructions (like 'let alone').
It addresses the open question of whether open-source models possess robust constructional understanding, a key area for AI democratization.
The study introduces a novel dataset specifically designed to test the meaning of rare Paired-Focus constructions in English.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the core LLM challenge of long-context reasoning, where models struggle with extensive, distracting information.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve this capability.
Overcomes limitations of prior work by providing supervision for intermediate reasoning steps, rather than just relying on sparse, final outcome rewards.

stories.title: What Gets Unmasked First? Trajectory Analysis of Diffusion Models for Graph-to-Text Generation
stories.author: Chengkai Li
bullet_points: Introduces a systematic study of Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) for graph-to-text generation.
Finds that MDLMs prioritize generating entities first, unlike linear autoregressive LLMs.
Identifies a new, previously undocumented failure mode in supervised fine-tuning of these models.

stories.title: What Am I Missing? Question-Answering as Hidden State Probing
stories.author: Xiaodan Zhu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the under-explored mechanisms of test-time reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs) following the rise of chain-of-thought prompting.
It proposes using question-asking as an inference-time intervention to probe and articulate information about the model's internal hidden state.
This research aims to provide deeper insights into how LLMs arrive at their varied answers, even from the same input.

stories.title: Semantic Triplet Restoration: A Novel Protocol for Hierarchical Table Understanding in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yuqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of table question answering in LLMs, which struggles with implicit semantic relations in 2D layouts.
Proposes Semantic Triplet Restoration (STR), a novel protocol to rewrite cells into atomic semantic triplets.
Aims to overcome limitations of current methods that rely on markup-heavy serializations like HTML or Markdown.

stories.title: Preference-Aware Rubric Learning for Personalized Evaluation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Personalization is a critical bottleneck in aligning evolving LLMs with individual user preferences.
Current evaluation methods (automatic metrics, LLM-as-a-judge) fail to capture subjective, user-specific preferences.
The research proposes new principles for reliable evaluation tailored to personalized alignment.

stories.title: UniAudio-Token: Empowering Semantic Speech Tokenizers with General Audio Perception
stories.author: Xiao Zhou
bullet_points: UniAudio-Token addresses the 'acoustic blindness' in semantic speech tokenizers used by Audio-LLMs.
The framework aims to integrate general audio perception capabilities into existing semantic tokenizers without sacrificing speech performance.
This work seeks to broaden the applicability of speech-centric tokenizers beyond purely linguistic tasks.

stories.title: The newest Instagram “exploit” is the goofiest I've seen
stories.author: ssiddharth
bullet_points: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support bot to compromise Instagram accounts.
The exploit leverages the AI system, suggesting vulnerabilities in automated support infrastructure.
The incident is highlighted as a notable, albeit 'goofy,' security breach.
